item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure that anybody would say 'stop' to somebody repeating the word 'sick' over and over again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not just nightmare fuel, but interesting nightmare fuel.
Theological appropriation aside, nice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Getting some real Junji Ito vibes from this, especially with the tower. Really like the more subtle religious elements, and overall the more understated nature of the piece - it begins as a sort of classic infection/body squick SCP, but then takes on a grander narrative.

+1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have two unsourced images within your article. Please source these images by replying to the sent PM and/or this post within 48 hours or they will have to be deleted. Also, make sure the images comply with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license by following this handy guide, otherwise they will have to be deleted.

48 hours have elapsed, and thus, the image(s) have been removed. Please PM with the source if you wish to have them restored or if you have any questions. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is this like some anti-tower of babble thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One question: why are infected humans designated -C? Why not -A or -1? It doesn't affect my enjoyment of the article, I'm just curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this and SCP-3305: The Father, The Son, and The Holy Toast back to back, and found the religious elements to be much better integrated in 4038. Subtler. Less "hand-holdy," and with more of a purpose to their inclusion.

3305 is like "GET IT GUYS? GET IT? DID YOU SEE WHAT I DID THERE!?" Meanwhile 4038 just puts the pieces in a shoebox and silently slides it down the table to you and waits patiently while you put the pieces together. Absolutely devastating. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ihp does have a point. I think this fits much better as Keter considering its contagious and we don't know where it comes from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it starts out completely random but makes sense at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thank you for your comments! I've edited them in.

As for object class: I've read quite a few scps by now and I'm still not entirely sure how to make the call (I know about the box test). Like 096 is euclid despite being a massive deadly breach risk, what's up with that? I went with euclid because though the skip is out there, it's relatively easy to secure and contain. I don't see the 'Uncontained' class get much use, but it may be in order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so disturbing in my mind's eye, I love it. Reminds me of something out of a Carpenter movie.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is weird enough for me to upvote, but I did notice a few things that could use adjustment:

SCP-4038-C will then begin muttering in their sleep in a polyglot mixture of approximately 150 languages, some of which have long been extinct

"When asleep, SCP-4038-C will begin muttering a polyglot mixture of approximately 150 languages some believed long extinct." sounds a lot better.

appears to be highly correlated with the numbers of languages the SCP-4038-C individual speaks

Should be "Number of languages"

However, upon being touched by a waking human the tongue begins to writhe in seeming panic before expiring

How does a tongue die? Does the tissue undergo actual necrosis? Or does it just fall back into the mouth, which can result in the tongue being swallowed?

One last quibble: why isn't this Keter-class? The vector of infection isn't known, and who's to say the infection at the music festival was an isolated occurrence? This could easily breach containment.

Still, this is weird and awesome. Tongues hating humans? +1.
